Patch Set 9: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(7 inline comments)

I guess we can change ordering in OFD so that lasted is updated before actual creation and update it to actual values in a rare error case.
Also, I tend to think it makes sense to lock lastid object in LFSCK/OFD so that we don't get partially updated value (for safety, though this is very unlikely).

Patch Set 9: (7 inline comments)

>I guess we can change ordering in OFD so that lasted is updated before actual creation and update it to actual values in a rare error case. Also, I tend to think it makes sense to lock lastid object in LFSCK/OFD so that we don't get partially updated value (for safety, though this is very unlikely).

The real trouble now is that someone may create OST-object and update LAST_ID after the LFSCK loading the LAST_ID file in RAM already. Even though we can change the order in OFD, such case still can happen unless all the updating LAST_ID file will be done by LFSCK only. But it makes the module relationship is more dirty...
Patch Set 9: (6 inline comments)

Patch Set 9: (3 inline comments)
Patch Set 9: (3 inline comments)

Patch Set 10: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(3 inline comments)

I think couple things are missing in the patch:
1) OFD doesn't reload LASTID after rebuilt?
2) there is some resynchronization needed between OST and MDT? (though I'm not absolutely sure it's in the scope of the patch)

say, latest precreated ID known to MDT is 1000. LFSCK reset it to 2000. at some point MDT asks to precreate objects upto 1500, OFD finds 2000 in LASTID (cached) and just returns 1500 as the last precreated. MDT starts to re-assign IDs in use?

probably OFD should return an error to precreate request after rebuilt, so that MDT can resync.

commit.subject: LU-1267 lfsck: rebuild LAST_ID
commit.message:
LU-1267 lfsck: rebuild LAST_ID

The /O/<seq>/LAST_ID records the last oid of the object allocated
within the sequence. The LAST_ID file can be crashed or missed as
the system running. The LFSCK for layout consistency verification
can detect the LAST_ID lost/crashed cases, and can rebuild it via
scanning the whole device.

This functionality is also part of LU-14 live replacement of OST.

Introduce lfsck_notify callback - the LFSCK events notification
channel from the LFSCK to the registered users (MDD/OFD).

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
